Policy -> Industry Collapse.change_reason: CORRELATION (highest achievable without refutation battery). Industry collapse is well-documented (92-96% closures) but was not tested with the analysis's own refutation battery. Carried forward from literature synthesis.

Industry Collapse -> Reduced Tutoring.change_reason: Compositional analysis shows education share dropped 0.7pp but z-score is only -1.05 (not unique among categories). Direction is consistent but effect is not distinguishable from general shifts.

Reduced Tutoring -> Total Expenditure.change_reason: Per-birth normalization eliminates the apparent level shift (p=0.48). Any aggregate decline is attributable to demographic decline, not per-child spending reduction.

Policy -> Underground Market.change_reason: HYPOTHESIZED: mechanical truth update applied (min(0.3, 0.3 - 0.1) = 0.2). Beyond analytical horizon: no systematic data to test.

Underground -> Higher Prices.change_reason: HYPOTHESIZED: mechanical truth update applied (min(0.3, 0.3 - 0.1) = 0.2). Beyond analytical horizon: no systematic data to test.

Competitive Pressure -> Inelastic Demand.change_reason: Not directly tested. CIEFR-HS evidence is pre-policy only.

Income -> Differential Access.change_reason: Urban ITS shift (-711) is 3.7x larger than rural (-191), consistent with higher urban exposure. But parallel trends are violated, so this is descriptive only.

Public Spending -> Crowding-In.change_reason: HYPOTHESIZED: mechanical truth update applied (min(0.3, 0.6 - 0.1) = 0.3). Lightweight assessment only. Public spending maintained above 4% GDP but no causal test possible.

Policy -> Aggregate Spending (net).change_reason: CORRELATION: 2/3 core refutation tests passed, jackknife FAIL (max dev 51.9%), COVID placebo FAIL. ITS & BSTS agree in direction (-23.7% vs -18.8%) but COVID confounding is severe. Relevance decreased due to COVID confounding.
